revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[as:চাৰিজন স্বৰ্গীয় ৰজা]][[bxr:Дүрбэн махаранза]][[cs:Čtyři nebeští králové]][[en:Four Heavenly Kings]][[es:Cuatro reyes celestiales]][[et:Maailmakaitsja]][[fr:Quatre Rois célestes]][[id:Catur Maharaja Kayika]][[ja:四天王]][[ko:사천왕]][[lt:Čaturmaharadža]][[ms:Empat Maharaja Langit]][[my:စတုမဟာရာဇ်နတ်မင်းကြီးလေးပါး]][[new:चतुर्महाराज]][[nl:Vier Hemelse Koningen]][[ro:Cei patru regi cerești]][[ru:Четыре Небесных Царя]][[simple:Four Heavenly Kings]][[si:සතරවරම් දෙවිවරු]][[sl:Čhatumaharadžika]][[ta:சதுர்மகாராசாக்கள்]][[th:จาตุมหาราชิกา]][[tl:Apat na Hari sa Langit]][[tr:Dört Göksel Kral]][[uk:Чотири небесні королі]][[vi:Thiên vương]][[zh-yue:四大天王]][[zh:四大天王]] {{Short description|Buddhist gods}} {{About|Buddhist gods}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=April 2015}} {{cleanup|date=January 2012}} }} ) at [[Tōdai-ji]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}]] depiction.]] The '''Four Heavenly Kings''' are four {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhist|1=Buddhism}} gods or {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|qid=Q4089509|lt=''devas''|1=Deva (Buddhism)}}, each of whom is believed to watch over one {{ill|Cardinal direction|en|Cardinal direction|qid=Q23718|lt=Cardinal direction|1=Cardinal direction}} of the world. The {{ill|Hall of Four Heavenly Kings|en|Hall of Four Heavenly Kings|qid=Q2680997|lt=Hall of Four Heavenly Kings|1=Hall of Four Heavenly Kings}} is a standard component of {{ill|Chinese Buddhism|en|Chinese Buddhism|qid=Q847618|lt=Chinese Buddhist|1=Chinese Buddhism}} {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=temples|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}}. {{anchor|Nomenclature|Etymology}} ==Names== The Kings are collectively named as follows: {| class="wikitable" |- !Language !Written form !Romanization !Translation |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|qid=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} |चतुर्महाराज |Chaturmahārāja
Chaturmahārājikā
|Four Great Kings |- |लोकपाल |{{ill|en:Lokapala|en|en:Lokapala|qid=Q1094074|lt=Lokapāla|1=Lokapala}} |Guardians of the World |- |{{ill|Sinhala language|en|Sinhala language|qid=Q13267|lt=Sinhala|1=Sinhala language}} |{{lang|si|සතරවරම් දෙවිවරු}} |{{transliteration|si|Satharawaram Dewi}} |Four Privileged/Bestowed Gods |- |{{ill|Burmese language|en|Burmese language|qid=Q9228|lt=Burmese|1=Burmese language}} |{{my|စတုလောကပါလ}}
{{my|စတုမဟာရာဇ်နတ်}} |{{IPA-my|sətṵ lɔ́ka̰ pàla̰|IPA}}
{{IPA-my|sətṵ məhà ɹɪʔ naʔ|IPA}} | Loanword from ''catulokapāla''
loanword from ''catumahā'' + king [[Nat (spirit)|nat]]s |- |rowspan=4|{{ill|Chinese language|en|Chinese language|qid=Q7850|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese language}} | {{lang|zh|天王}} | {{transliteration|zh|Tiānwáng}} | Heavenly Kings |- | {{lang|zh|四天王}} | {{transliteration|zh|Sìtiānwáng}} | Four Heavenly Kings |- | {{lang|zh|四大天王}} | {{transliteration|zh|Sìdà Tiānwáng}} | Four Great Heavenly Kings |- | {{lang|zh|风调雨顺/風調雨順}} | {{transliteration|zh|Fēng Tiáo Yǔ Shùn}} | Good, rainy weather for growing crops |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|qid=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}} | {{lang|ja|四天王}} | {{transliteration|ja|Shi Tennō}} | Four Heavenly Kings |- | {{lang|ja|四大天王}} | {{transliteration|ja|Shidai Tennō}} | Four Great Heavenly Kings |- |{{ill|Korean language|en|Korean language|qid=Q9176|lt=Korean|1=Korean language}} | {{lang|ko|四天王}}/사천왕 |{{transliteration|ko|Sa-cheonwang}} | Four heavenly kings |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Vietnamese language|en|Vietnamese language|qid=Q9199|lt=Vietnamese|1=Vietnamese language}} | 四天王 |Tứ Thiên Vương | Four heavenly kings |- | 四大天王 |Tứ Đại Thiên Vương | Four great heavenly kings |- |{{ill|Tagalog language|en|Tagalog language|qid=Q34057|lt=Tagalog|1=Tagalog language}} | ᜀᜉᜆ᜔ᜈᜑᜇᜒ ᜐᜎᜅᜒᜆ᜔ | Apat na Hari sa Langit | Four heavenly kings |- |[[:en:Standard Tibetan|Tibetan]] |རྒྱལ༌ཆེན༌བཞི༌ |rgyal chen bzhi | Four great kings |- |{{ill|Mongolian language|en|Mongolian language|qid=Q9246|lt=Mongolian|1=Mongolian language}} |{{MongolUnicode|ᠳᠥᠷᠪᠡ
ᠮᠠᠬᠠᠷᠠᠨᠵᠠ|}}
Тэнгэрийн дөрвөн хаан |Tengeriin dörwön xaan | Four kings of the sky |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Thai language|en|Thai language|qid=Q9217|lt=Thai|1=Thai language}} | จาตุมหาราชา | Chatumaharacha | Four Great Kings, loan word from catumahārāja (Pali) |- | จตุโลกบาล | Chatulokkaban | Four Guardians of the World, loan word from catulokapāla (Pali) |- |{{ill|Pali|en|Pali|qid=Q36727|lt=Pali|1=Pali}} |Catu-Mahārāja |Catu-Mahārāja |The Four Great Kings |} Individually, they have different names and features. {| class="wikitable" |- |{{ill|Pali|en|Pali|qid=Q36727|lt=Pali|1=Pali}} name |Vessavana |Virūlhaka |Dhatarattha |Virūpakkha |- |{{ill|Devanagari|en|Devanagari|qid=Q38592|lt=Devanagari|1=Devanagari}}
{{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|qid=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}} romanization |{{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=वैश्रवण (कुबेर)
{{IAST|Vaiśravaṇa}}}} (Kubera) |{{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|qid=Q1188515|lt=विरूढक
{{IAST|Virūḍhaka}}}} |{{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|qid=Q1188767|lt=धृतराष्ट्र
{{IAST|Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}}} |{{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|qid=Q1188714|lt=विरूपाक्ष
{{IAST|Virūpākṣa}}}} |- |Meaning |He who hears everything |He who causes to grow |He who upholds the realm |He who sees all |- |Control |{{ill|Yaksha|en|Yaksha|qid=Q6011093|lt=yakkhas|1=Yaksha}} |{{ill|Kumbhanda|en|Kumbhanda|qid=Q6132115|lt=Kumbhanda|1=Kumbhanda}}s |{{ill|Gandharva|en|Gandharva|qid=Q1291498|lt=gandhabbas|1=Gandharva}} |[[Nāga|nagas]] |- | Description | This is the chief of the four kings and protector of the north. He is the ruler of {{ill|Rain|en|Rain|qid=Q7925|lt=Rain|1=Rain}}. His symbolic weapons are the {{ill|Umbrella|en|Umbrella|qid=Q41607|lt=Umbrella|1=Umbrella}} or {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|qid=Q199451|lt=Pagoda|1=Pagoda}}. Wearing heavy armor and carrying the umbrella in his right hand, he is often associated with the ancient {{ill|Sri Lanka|en|Sri Lanka|qid=Q854|lt=Sri Lanka|1=Sri Lanka}}n god of wealth, {{ill|Kubera|en|Kubera|qid=Q223617|lt=Kubera|1=Kubera}}. Associated with the color yellow or green. | King of the south and one who causes good growth of roots. He is the ruler of the wind. His symbolic weapon is the sword which he carries in his right hand to protect the Dharma and the {{ill|Jambudvīpa|en|Jambudvīpa|qid=Q1586228|lt=southern continent|1=Jambudvīpa}}. Associated with the color blue. | King of the east and god of music. His symbolic weapon is the {{ill|Pipa|en|Pipa|qid=Q6685124|lt=Pipa|1=Pipa}} (stringed instrument). He is harmonious and compassionate and protects all beings. Uses his music to convert others to Buddhism. Associated with the color white. | King of the west and one who sees all. His symbolic weapon is a snake or red cord that is representative of a dragon. As the eye in the sky, he sees people who do not believe in Buddhism and converts them. His ancient name means "he who has broad objectives". Associated with the color red |- |image | | | | |- |Color |yellow or green |blue |white |red |- | rowspan="3" |Symbol |umbrella | rowspan="3" |sword | rowspan="3" |{{ill|Pipa|en|Pipa|qid=Q6685124|lt=Pipa|1=Pipa}} |serpent |- |mongoose |[[stupa]] |- |stupa |pearl |- |Followers |{{ill|Yaksha|en|Yaksha|qid=Q6011093|lt={{IAST|yakṣas}}}} |{{ill|Kumbhanda|en|Kumbhanda|qid=Q6132115|lt={{IAST|kumbhāṇḍas}}}} |{{ill|Gandharva|en|Gandharva|qid=Q1291498|lt=Gandharva|1=Gandharva}}s |[[nāga]]s |- |Direction |north |south |east |west |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Traditional Chinese characters|en|Traditional Chinese characters|qid=Q178528|lt=Traditional|1=Traditional Chinese characters}}/{{ill|Simplified Chinese characters|en|Simplified Chinese characters|qid=Q185614|lt=Simplified|1=Simplified Chinese characters}} Chinese
{{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|qid=Q42222|lt=Pinyin|1=Pinyin}} |{{lang|zh|多聞天王}} / {{lang|zh|多闻天王}}
{{lang|zh-Latn|Duōwén Tiānwáng}} |{{lang|zh|增長天王}} / {{lang|zh|增长天王}}
{{lang|zh-Latn|Zēngzhǎng Tiānwáng}} |{{lang|zh|持國天王}} / {{lang|zh|持国天王}}
{{lang|zh-Latn|Chíguó Tiānwáng}} |{{lang|zh|廣目天王}} / {{lang|zh|广目天王}}
{{lang|zh-Latn|Guăngmù Tiānwáng}} |- |{{lang|zh|毗沙門天}} / {{lang|zh|毗沙门天}} |{{lang|zh|留博叉天}} / {{lang|zh|留博叉天}} |{{lang|zh|多羅吒天}} / {{lang|zh|多罗吒天}} |{{lang|zh|毗琉璃天}} / {{lang|zh|毗琉璃天}} |- |rowspan=2|{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}
{{ill|Hepburn romanization|en|Hepburn romanization|qid=Q667558|lt=Hepburn romanization|1=Hepburn romanization}} |rowspan=2|{{lang|ja|多聞天}} ({{lang|ja|毘沙門天}})
{{lang|ja-Latn|Tamon-ten}} ({{lang|ja-Latn|Bishamon-ten}}) |rowspan=2|{{lang|ja|増長天}}
{{lang|ja-Latn|Zōchō-ten}} |{{lang|ja|持国天}}
{{lang|ja-Latn|Jikoku-ten}} |rowspan=2|{{lang|ja|広目天}}
{{lang|ja-Latn|Kōmoku-ten}} |- |{{lang|ja|治国天}}
{{lang|ja-Latn|Jikoku-ten}} |- |{{ill|Hangul|en|Hangul|qid=Q8222|lt=Hangul|1=Hangul}}
{{ill|Revised Romanization of Korean|en|Revised Romanization of Korean|qid=Q498721|lt=romanized Korean|1=Revised Romanization of Korean}} |{{lang|ko|다문천왕}}
{{lang|ko-Latn|Damun-cheonwang}} |{{lang|ko|증장천왕}}
增長天王
{{lang|ko-Latn|Jeungjang-cheonwang}} |{{lang|ko|지국천왕}}
持國天王
{{lang|ko-Latn|Jiguk-cheonwang}} |{{lang|ko|광목천왕}}
廣目天王
{{lang|ko-Latn|Gwangmok-cheonwang}} |- |{{ill|Vietnamese alphabet|en|Vietnamese alphabet|qid=Q622712|lt=Vietnamese alphabet|1=Vietnamese alphabet}}
{{ill|Chữ Hán|en|Chữ Hán|qid=Q1378119|lt=Chữ Hán|1=Chữ Hán}} |{{lang|vi|Đa Văn Thiên Vương}}
多聞天王 |{{lang|vi|Tăng Trưởng Thiên Vương}}
增長天王 |{{lang|vi|Trì Quốc Thiên Vương}}
持國天王 |{{lang|vi|Quảng Mục Thiên Vương}}
廣目天王 |- |{{ill|Filipino alphabet|en|Filipino alphabet|qid=Q278446|lt=Filipino alphabet|1=Filipino alphabet}}
{{ill|Baybayin|en|Baybayin|qid=Q812124|lt=Baybayin|1=Baybayin}} |{{lang|tl|Bisrabana}}
ᜊᜀᜒᜐᜀᜊᜀᜈᜀ |{{lang|tl|Birudhaka}}
ᜊᜒᜓᜇᜑᜀᜃᜀ |{{lang|tl|Dhltalastla}}
ᜇᜑᜎᜆᜑᜀᜎᜀᜐᜆᜎᜀ |{{lang|tl|Bilupaksa}}
ᜊᜒᜎᜓᜉᜀᜃᜐᜀ |- |{{ill|Burmese alphabet|en|Burmese alphabet|qid=Q854968|lt=Burmese Script|1=Burmese alphabet}} | ဝေဿဝဏ္ဏနတ်မင်း (''Waithawun Nat Min'')
ကုဝေရနတ်မင်း (''Kuwaira Nat Min'') | ဝိရူဠကနတ်မင်း (''Wirulakka Nat Min'') | ဓတရဋ္ဌနတ်မင်း (''Datarattha Nat Min'') | ဝိရူပက္ခနတ်မင်း (''Wirupakkha Nat Min'') |- |[[:en:Tibetan alphabet|Tibetan alphabet]] and {{ill|THL Simplified Phonetic Transcription|en|THL Simplified Phonetic Transcription|qid=Q650858|lt=romanization|1=THL Simplified Phonetic Transcription}} |རྣམ་ཐོས་སྲས་ (Namthöse) |ཕགས་སྐྱེས་པོ་ (Phakyepo) |ཡུལ་འཁོར་སྲུང་ (Yülkhorsung) |སྤྱན་མི་བཟང་ (Chenmizang) |- |[[Mongolian Script]] and [[Mongolian Cyrillic]] and [[Mongolian Latin alphabet]] |{{MongolUnicode|ᠥᠯᠥᠨ ᠦᠨᠳᠡᠰᠲᠨᠢᠢ ᠦᠽᠡᠯ}}
(Олон үндэстний үзэл)
{{lang|mn-Latn|Olon ündestnii üzel}} |{{MongolUnicode|ᠲᠢᠶᠡᠨᠢ ᠥᠰᠥᠯᠲ}}
(Тиений өсөлт)
{{lang|mn-Latn|Tiyenii ösölt}} |{{MongolUnicode|ᠦᠨᠳᠡᠰᠲᠨᠢᠢ ᠽᠠᠰᠤᠠᠷ ᠦᠯᠢᠴᠬᠢᠯᠭᠡᠡ}}
(үндэсний засвар үйлчилгээ)
{{lang|mn-Latn|ündesnii zasvar üilchilgee}} |{{MongolUnicode|ᠰᠶᠡᠯᠶᠡᠰᠲᠢᠶᠡᠯ ᠰᠦᠷᠲᠠᠯᠴᠬᠢᠯᠭᠠᠠ}}
(селестиел сурталчилгаа)
{{lang|mn-Latn|syelyestiyel surtalchilgaa}} |- |{{ill|Thai script|en|Thai script|qid=Q236376|lt=Thai script|1=Thai script}}
{{ill|Royal Thai General System of Transcription|en|Royal Thai General System of Transcription|qid=Q1268261|lt=romanization|1=Royal Thai General System of Transcription}} | ท้าวเวสวัณ (''Thao Wetsawan'')
ท้าวเวสสุวรรณ (''Thao Wetsuwan'')
ท้าวกุเวร (''Thao Kuwen'') | ท้าววิรุฬหก (''Thao Wirunhok'') | ท้าวธตรฐ (''Thao Thatarot'') | ท้าววิรูปักษ์ (''Thao Wirupak'') |} File:Guardian of Phra Meru Mas of Bhumibol Adulyadej - Vessavana (right side).jpg|Vaiśravaṇa of the north direction, king of yakṣas. File:Guardian of Phra Meru Mas of Bhumibol Adulyadej - Virulhaka (right side).jpg|Virūḍhaka of the south direction, king of kumbhāṇḍas. File:Guardian of Phra Meru Mas of Bhumibol Adulyadej - Dhatarattha (left side).jpg|Dhṛtarāṣṭra of the east direction, king of gandharvas. File:Guardian of Phra Meru Mas of Bhumibol Adulyadej - Virupakkha (left side).jpg|Virūpākṣa of the west direction, king of nāgas. ==Mythology== All four Kings serve {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|qid=Q1444745|lt={{IAST|Śakra}}}}, the lord of the devas of {{IAST|{{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|qid=Q1360289|lt=Trāyastriṃśa|1=Trāyastriṃśa}}}}. On the 8th, 14th and 15th days of each {{ill|Lunar month|en|Lunar month|qid=Q591259|lt=Lunar month|1=Lunar month}}, the Kings either send out emissaries or go themselves to inspect the state of virtue and morality in the world of men. Then they report their findings to the assembly of the {{IAST|Trāyastriṃśa}} devas. On the orders of Śakra, the Kings and their retinues stand guard to protect {{IAST|Trāyastriṃśa}} from another attack by the [[Asura (Buddhism)|Asuras]], which once threatened to destroy the realm of the devas. They also vowed to protect the Buddha, the {{ill|Dharma (Buddhism)|en|Dharma (Buddhism)|qid=Q9241518|lt=Dharma|1=Dharma (Buddhism)}}, and the Buddha's followers from danger. In Chinese Buddhism, all four of the heavenly kings are regarded as four of the ''{{ill|Twenty-Four Protective Deities|en|Twenty-Four Protective Deities|qid=Q15912611|lt=Twenty Devas|1=Twenty-Four Protective Deities}}'' (二十諸天 Èrshí Zhūtiān) or the ''{{ill|Twenty-Four Protective Deities|en|Twenty-Four Protective Deities|qid=Q15912611|lt=Twenty-Four Devas|1=Twenty-Four Protective Deities}}'' (二十四諸天 Èrshísì zhūtiān), a group of Buddhist dharmapalas who manifest to protect the Dharma.{{Cite book|url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/275253538|title=A dictionary of Chinese Buddhist terms : with Sanskrit and English equivalents and a Sanskrit-Pali index|date=2004|publisher=RoutledgeCurzon|others=Lewis Hodous, William Edward Soothill|isbn=0-203-64186-8|location=London|oclc=275253538}} in {{ill|Beijing|en|Beijing|qid=Q956|lt=Beijing, China|1=Beijing}}.]] According to {{ill|Vasubandhu|en|Vasubandhu|qid=Q316343|lt=Vasubandhu|1=Vasubandhu}}, devas born in the Cāturmahārājika heaven are 1/4 of a [[krośa]] in height (about 750 feet tall). They have a five-hundred-year lifespan, of which each day is equivalent to 50 years in our world; thus their total lifespan amounts to about nine million years (other sources say 90,000 years). The attributes borne by each King also link them to their followers; for instance, the [[nāga]]s, magical creatures who can change form between human and serpent, are led by {{IAST|Virūpākṣa}}, represented by a snake; the {{ill|Gandharva|en|Gandharva|qid=Q1291498|lt=Gandharva|1=Gandharva}}s are celestial musicians, led by {{IAST|Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, represented with a lute. The umbrella was a symbol of regal sovereignty in ancient India, and the sword is a symbol of martial prowess. {{IAST|Vaiśravaṇa}}'s mongoose, which ejects jewels from its mouth, is said to represent generosity in opposition to greed. {|border="0" cellspacing="5" | |align = "center"|''{{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Vaiśravaṇa|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}'' (north) | |- |align = "center"|''{{ill|Virūpākṣa|en|Virūpākṣa|qid=Q1188714|lt=Virūpākṣa|1=Virūpākṣa}}'' (west) |align = "center"|'''Heavenly Kings''' |align = "center"|''{{ill|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|en|Dhṛtarāṣṭra|qid=Q1188767|lt=Dhṛtarāṣṭra|1=Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}'' (east) |- | |align = "center"|{{ill|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|en|Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)|qid=Q1188515|lt=''Virūḍhaka''|1=Virūḍhaka (Heavenly King)}} (south) | |} ==Gallery== File:Lingyin temple 06.jpg|Duōwén Tiānwáng (north) File:Hangzhou-Lingyin-Tempel-04-Waechter-2012-gje.jpg|Zēngzhǎng Tiānwáng (south) File:Lingyin temple 04.jpg|Chíguó Tiānwáng (east) File:Lingyin temple 07.jpg|Guăngmù Tiānwáng (west) Image:Jikoji jikokuten.JPG|Jikoku-ten (east) Image:Jikoji zochoten.JPG|Zōjō-ten (south) Image:Jikoji komokuten.JPG|Kōmoku-ten (west) Image:Jikoji tamonten.JPG|Tamon-ten (north) ==Popular culture== *In the Hong Kong entertainment industry, {{ill|Andy Lau|en|Andy Lau|qid=Q16766|lt=Andy Lau|1=Andy Lau}}, {{ill|Jacky Cheung|en|Jacky Cheung|qid=Q16781|lt=Jacky Cheung|1=Jacky Cheung}}, {{ill|Leon Lai|en|Leon Lai|qid=Q16836|lt=Leon Lai|1=Leon Lai}}, {{ill|Aaron Kwok|en|Aaron Kwok|qid=Q16872|lt=Aaron Kwok|1=Aaron Kwok}} are known as the Four Heavenly Kings. *In [[:en:Sailor Moon (season 1)|first seasons of ''Sailor Moon'']], and , the Four Heavenly Kings were the four loyal and faithfully devoted generals and bodyguards of Prince Endymion. *In {{ill|MegaBeast Investigator Juspion|en|MegaBeast Investigator Juspion|qid=Q1318230|lt=MegaBeast Investigator Juspion|1=MegaBeast Investigator Juspion}}, starting in episode 13, the main villain Mad Gallant employs a quartet of assassins called the Four Evil Heavenly Kings (悪の四天王, ''Aku no Shitennō''). *The third movie of Detective Dee, by Tsui Hark, "Detective Dee: The Four Heavenly Kings" (2018) (traditional Chinese: 狄仁傑之四大天王; simplified Chinese: 狄仁杰之四大天王). *In {{ill|Pokémon|en|Pokémon|qid=Q864|lt=Pokémon|1=Pokémon}}, the group of Pokémon trainers known as the [[:en:Elite Four|Elite Four]] in English are called the Four Heavenly Kings (四天王) in Japanese. *In {{ill|Street Fighter|en|Street Fighter|qid=Q288035|lt=Street Fighter|1=Street Fighter}}, the leading members of {{ill|Shadaloo|en|Shadaloo|qid=Q4387681|lt=Shadaloo|1=Shadaloo}} known as the [[Grand Masters]] in English are known as the Four Heavenly Kings (四天王). They consist of [[M. Bison]] (Vega in Japanese), {{ill|Vega (Street Fighter)|en|Vega (Street Fighter)|qid=Q1901282|lt=Vega|1=Vega (Street Fighter)}} (Balrog in Japanese), {{ill|Balrog (Street Fighter)|en|Balrog (Street Fighter)|qid=Q2479366|lt=Balrog|1=Balrog (Street Fighter)}} (M. Bison in Japanese), {{ill|Sagat (Street Fighter)|en|Sagat (Street Fighter)|qid=Q742181|lt=Sagat|1=Sagat (Street Fighter)}} (formerly), and {{ill|F.A.N.G. (Street Fighter)|en|F.A.N.G. (Street Fighter)|qid=Q104870632|lt=F.A.N.G.|1=F.A.N.G. (Street Fighter)}} ==See also== {{Portal|Religion}} *{{ill|Anemoi|en|Anemoi|qid=Q476682|lt=Anemoi|1=Anemoi}} *{{ill|Bacab|en|Bacab|qid=Q2465771|lt=Bacab|1=Bacab}} *[[:en:Norðri, Suðri, Austri and Vestri|Four Dwarves (Norse mythology)]] *{{ill|Four Holy Beasts|en|Four Holy Beasts|qid=Q837247|lt=Four Holy Beasts|1=Four Holy Beasts}} *[[Living creatures (Bible)|Four Living Creatures]] *{{ill|Four sons of Horus|en|Four sons of Horus|qid=Q727053|lt=Four sons of Horus|1=Four sons of Horus}} *{{ill|Dáinn, Dvalinn, Duneyrr and Duraþrór|en|Dáinn, Dvalinn, Duneyrr and Duraþrór|qid=Q1157627|lt=Four Stags (Norse mythology)|1=Dáinn, Dvalinn, Duneyrr and Duraþrór}} *{{ill|Four Symbols|en|Four Symbols|qid=Q1073260|lt=Four Symbols|1=Four Symbols}} *{{ill|Four temperaments|en|Four temperaments|qid=Q638210|lt=Four temperaments|1=Four temperaments}} *{{ill|Guardians of the directions|en|Guardians of the directions|qid=Q1398903|lt=Guardians of the directions|1=Guardians of the directions}} *{{ill|en:Lokapala|en|en:Lokapala|qid=Q1094074|lt=en:Lokapala|1=Lokapala}} *{{ill|Royal stars|en|Royal stars|qid=Q3592536|lt=Royal stars|1=Royal stars}} *[[:en:Svetovid|Svetovid]] *{{ill|Tetramorph|en|Tetramorph|qid=Q2010254|lt=Tetramorph|1=Tetramorph}} *{{ill|Watchtower (magic)|en|Watchtower (magic)|qid=Q7973183|lt=Watchtower|1=Watchtower (magic)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{refbegin}} * Chaudhuri, Saroj Kumar. ''Hindu Gods and Goddesses in Japan''. New Delhi: Vedams eBooks (P) Ltd., 2003. {{ISBN|81-7936-009-1}}. * Nakamura, Hajime. ''Japan and Indian Asia: Their Cultural Relations in the Past and Present''. Calcutta: Firma K.L. Mukhopadhyay, 1961. Pp. 1–31. * Potter, Karl H., ed. ''India and Japan: A Study in Interaction During 5th cent.–14th cent. A.D.''. New Delhi: Abhinav Publications, 1992. {{ISBN|81-7017-289-6}}. ''Digital Dictionary of Buddhism in Japan''. {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Buddhist Pantheon}} {{Buddhism topics}} {{Burmese nats}} [[File:Todaiji05s3200.jpg|thumb|Tamon-ten ({{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Vaiśravaṇa|1=Vaiśravaṇa}} [[File:Four Guardian Kings in Burmese art.jpg|thumb|The Four Guardian Kings in {{ill|Myanmar|en|Myanmar|qid=Q836|lt=Burmese|1=Myanmar}} [[File:Vaisravana..jpg|194px|center|Chief of the four kings and protector of the north]] [[File:Virudhaka.jpg|200px|center|King of the south and one who causes good growth of roots]] [[File:Dhrtarastra.jpg|200px|center|King of the east and god of music]] [[File:Virupaksa.jpg|200px|center|King of the west and one who sees all]] [[File:Fourheavenlykings4096x1360.jpg|center|thumb|575x575px|Statues of the Four Heavenly Kings. From left to right: {{IAST|Vaiśravaṇa}}, {{IAST|Virūḍhaka}}, {{IAST|Dhṛtarāṣṭra}}, and {{IAST|Virūpākṣa}} in {{ill|Beihai Park|en|Beihai Park|qid=Q133650|lt=Beihai Park|1=Beihai Park}} [[File:Chomyo Komokuten.jpg|thumb|right|Painting of Kōmokuten ({{IAST|Virūpākṣa}}), the Guardian of the West (one of the Four Guardian Kings). 13th century, Japan.]] [[List of Sailor Moon Crystal episodes#Season 1: Dark Kingdom (2014)|''Sailor Moon Crystal'']] [[en:Four Heavenly Kings]] [[as:চাৰিজন স্বৰ্গীয় ৰজা]] [[bxr:Дүрбэн махаранза]] {{Commons category|Four Heavenly Kings}} [[cs:Čtyři nebeští králové]] [[es:Cuatro reyes celestiales]] [[et:Maailmakaitsja]] [[fr:Quatre Rois célestes]] [[id:Catur Maharaja Kayika]] [[ja:四天王]] [[ko:사천왕]] [[lt:Čaturmaharadža]] [[ms:Empat Maharaja Langit]] [[my:စတုမဟာရာဇ်နတ်မင်းကြီးလေးပါး]] [[new:चतुर्महाराज]] [[nl:Vier Hemelse Koningen]] [[ro:Cei patru regi cerești]] [[ru:Четыре Небесных Царя]] [[si:සතරවරම් දෙවිවරු]] [[simple:Four Heavenly Kings]] [[sl:Čhatumaharadžika]] [[ta:சதுர்மகாராசாக்கள்]] [[th:จาตุมหาราชิกา]] [[tr:Dört Göksel Kral]] [[uk:Чотири небесні королі]] [[vi:Thiên vương]] [[zh:四大天王]] [[zh-yue:四大天王]] {{wikidata link|Q1068367}} [[Category:Buddhist cosmology]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Dharmapalas]] [[Category:Four Heavenly Kings]] [[Category:Four Heavenly Kings| ]] [[Category:Journey to the West characters]] [[Category:Vajrayana deities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
